strengths: 15+ years fullstack experience with core stack match: TypeScript, Node.js, React, MongoDB, and AWS CDK in recent production roles
Direct AI agent development experience — MCP code generators and agentic workflow frameworks at current role, explicitly required by the JD
Strong engineering craft and communication: TDD/CI/CD/trunk-based development practice, authored testing libraries, and delivered public talks and workshops
concerns: No Nest.js experience mentioned, which is the primary backend framework in the stack
No explicit Vitest/Jest/Playwright usage named despite broad testing background; no Python or Kotlin (Java only briefly)
No explicit authentication/authorization or security best-practices experience cited on the resume

key_factors.factor: Missing Nest.js and named testing frameworks

response.justification: The role's core competencies are TypeScript/Node/React fullstack delivery, testing rigor, cloud/containerization, polyglot backend ability, and AI agent development. The candidate evidences fullstack depth (Optilyz: TypeScript, React, MongoDB, AWS CDK), containerization and Kubernetes (GISCollective deployments), testing rigor (TDD at RIDE Capital, authored Fluent-Asserts and Trial test tooling), polyglot breadth (D, Java, C++, Swift), and current AI-agent infrastructure work at re:cinq (MCP code generators, agentic frameworks) — the rarest required skill. Gaps are Nest.js, the specific Vitest/Jest/Playwright frameworks, Python/Kotlin, and explicit security experience, all plausibly learnable given demonstrated framework-authoring ability but unevidenced. A CS bachelor's degree and public speaking/workshop record satisfy the core qualifications. Evidence strongly covers most competencies with a few named-technology gaps.
